what_it_does: This file is a research-wave findings report that documents the actual built state of the addressed-surface subsystem (mark-up, context auto-resolution, ui:// addressing, mockup handling, and walkthrough/review backend) and stress-tests each claim against the original anchor ideas, listing what is built, what is deferred, and what is net-new.
observations: The annotate / ingest_comment / attach_chat pipeline is real and separated by design: annotate writes annotations, ingest_comment adds one gold chat turn, attach_chat writes a chat turn, and set_presentation_pref stores control prefs without becoming twin gold.

R2 context resolution is fully implemented with five strata (annotations, presentation prefs, chats, events, howtos, plus run:// versions), a 4000-character hard budget, recency/proximity/pin/semantic scoring, and deduplication of triple-counted comments.
The RHM cannot call annotate directly because annotate is not in the RHM_VERBS whitelist; only request_change offers a conversational path, scoped to build intents.

Group addressing works only downward via the ancestor walk: a comment on a parent address resolves into child contexts, but standing at a group address does not roll up descendant or sibling comments.
mockup:// is a reading context for the RHM, not an annotatable address; annotations attach to the element's ui:// address whether the element is in a mockup or live DOM.
The screen-directory pattern is a flat naming convention plus register.json journey sequences, not a filesystem directory hierarchy.

The walkthrough/guide backend (start_session, present_current, next, start_walkthrough, start_guide) is built and model-free, but FE show-me lane wiring is deferred and per-step view-drive needs verification.
A status table maps each capability to built/deferred/not-built with evidence markers.
standouts: R2 is built and running with multi-stratum scoring and a hard 4000-character budget cap, which is stronger than the anchor described.
The RHM cannot call annotate mid-conversation because the verb is absent from RHM_VERBS; the gap is a whitelist/governance decision, not missing machinery.
Group annotation is directionally asymmetric: parent-to-child inheritance works, but child-to-parent rollup and sibling sharing do not.
mockup:// is only a reading context; the actual annotatable coordinate is always a ui:// address.
The guided-review backend exists, but the live conversational/streaming mode and the plan-generation step from accumulated mark-up are real net-new gaps.
conventions: Uses evidence markers Observed / Inferred / External / Anchor-idea consistently.
Cites file:line references for code claims.
Structures findings as numbered sections with subsections and a final status table.
Maintains a neutral, non-ranking tone while explicitly contradicting the anchor where the code differs.
Distinguishes built, deferred, and not-built capabilities with checkmark/cross symbols.
concerns: Heavy reliance on line-number citations means the report will drift as the codebase changes.
Some claims are marked Inferred rather than Observed, which may need re-verification.
The file is long and mixes factual inventory with prescriptive 'what should be built' suggestions, which could be split in a fused ledger.
notes: A code-grounded audit of how much of the guided-review addressed surface is already built versus still missing, with precise contradictions to the anchor design.
questions: Which non-studio front-end surfaces currently set a ui:// locus in focus.selected?
How will howto text be authored for the many element addresses that currently lack it?
What is the intended design for the plan-generation step from accumulated mark-up?
Will the RHM annotate verb default to current_locus() or require an explicit address?
Is there a planned descendant-gather for group rollup, or will that remain unsupported?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to report research findings for AREA 3, and the file does exactly that: it inventories the addressed surface, compares it to the anchor, and identifies gaps.
apparent_themes: Asymmetric tree inheritance for group addressing
Separation of reading context (mockup://) from annotatable coordinate (ui://)
intention_signals: The file is trying to give the team an honest, evidence-based map of what already works in the addressed-surface design so they can decide which gaps are wiring versus new building, especially for the guided-review experience.
novelty: Its distinct value is the directional analysis of group addressing (parent-to-child inheritance works, reverse rollup does not) and the precise separation of mockup:// reading context from ui:// annotation target.
contribution: A neutral, evidence-tagged inventory of built, deferred, and missing capabilities for the addressed surface, plus code-grounded contradictions that a fused system can use to scope the remaining guided-review work.
summary_for_embedding: A research findings report on AREA 3 documenting the actual implementation status of the addressed surface, including mark-up pipelines, R2 context resolution, ui:// addressing, mockup handling, and walkthrough backend, with a gap analysis against the anchor design.
intention_for_embedding: To provide an honest, code-grounded assessment of how much of the guided-review addressed surface exists and what still needs wiring or new building.
